title: Number patterns should be regular
description: Number patterns should be regularAnalyze your codeconsistency-formattedmaintainabilityCode SmellsuspiciousWhy is this an issue?The use of punctuation characters to separate subgroups in a number can make the number more readable. For instance consider 1,000,000,000 versus 1000000000. But when the grouping is irregular, such as 1,000,00,000; it indicates an error.This rule raises an issue when underscores (_) are used to break a number into irregular subgroups.Noncompliant code exampleint thousand = 100_0; int tenThousand = 100_00; int million = 1_000_00_000;Compliant solutionint thousand = 1000; int tenThousand = 10_000; int tenThousandWithout = 10000; int duos = 1_00_00; int million = 100_000_000;Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Se
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
int thousand = 100_0; // Noncompliant: irregular grouping
int tenThousand = 100_00; // Noncompliant: irregular grouping
int million = 1_000_00_000; // Noncompliant: irregular grouping
compliant:
int thousand = 1000; // No separators
int tenThousand = 10_000; // Regular grouping by thousands
int million = 100_000_000; // Regular grouping by thousands

logic.reasoning: Detects numeric literals with irregular underscore grouping patterns. Matches numbers with underscores but excludes regular patterns (3-digit groups like 1_000_000, or 2-digit cultural patterns). Also excludes hexadecimal, binary, floating point numbers, comments, strings, and test contexts where irregular patterns might be intentional for testing purposes.
